title: mappia senegalensis
intro: Mappia senegalensis, also known as the West African mappia or bitter leaf, is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antihypertensive effects. The plant's leaves and roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and lowered blood pressure. In herbal preparations, Mappia senegalensis is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, or extracts that are consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
